title: foundation — base compliance regime taxonomy
description: The regime taxonomy every downstream module's `compliance.json` references. Foundation itself produces no compliance evidence (it holds no personal data) — this file DEFINES the regimes + the control-language conventions, then every downstream module cites specific articles + evidence artefacts under these regimes.

gdpr.name: General Data Protection Regulation
gdpr.applicableWhen: Any EU/EEA data subject in the workspace's user base OR any processing that targets EU residents.

commonArticles.principle: storage limitation — data kept no longer than necessary
lawful basis — one of consent / contract / legal obligation / vital interest / public task / legitimate interest
conditions for consent — freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous, withdrawable
child's consent — parental consent required when subject is under member-state age of consent (13-16)
right of access — DSAR export within 1 month
right to erasure — 'right to be forgotten'
right to data portability — machine-readable export
right to object — especially for legitimate-interest basis
automated decision-making — no solely-automated decisions with significant effect without human review
data protection by design + default
processor obligations — DPA + subprocessor list
records of processing — including DPO contact
security — encryption, access control, breach detection
breach notification — 72h to supervisory authority
breach notification to data subjects — undue delay
DPIA — for high-risk processing (minors, biometrics, systematic monitoring)
DPO designation — required for public authorities + large-scale processing

commonEvidencePatterns: TOS acceptance timestamp on User creation → Art. 6(1)(b) lawful basis: contract
Compliance module's DSAR orchestrator aggregating across modules → Art. 15
WorkspaceContact where kind=dpo + P7Y retention hold → Art. 30
data-classes.json restricted tier + KMS encryption → Art. 32

uk_gdpr.name: UK GDPR
uk_gdpr.regulation: UK Data Protection Act 2018 + retained GDPR
uk_gdpr.applicableWhen: Same as GDPR but for UK data subjects. Post-Brexit variant with substantially identical controls.

commonArticles.principle: mirror of GDPR articles

commonEvidencePatterns: See GDPR — evidence artefacts are identical

coppa.name: Children's Online Privacy Protection Act
coppa.applicableWhen: Any child under 13 processed as a workspace user.

commonArticles.principle: notice to parents
storage limitation for children's data
verifiable parental consent (VPC) before collection
parental review + deletion right
confidentiality, security, integrity of children's PII
safe-harbor program participation (recommended)

commonEvidencePatterns: Registration flow age gate + VPC flow before minor User creation
Guardian consent notification via safeguarding module
No behavioural marketing to under-13 users (feature-flag gated)

ferpa.name: Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act
ferpa.jurisdiction: United States (schools receiving federal education funds)
ferpa.applicableWhen: Workspace is a school or higher-ed institution + processes student records.

commonArticles.principle: parent inspection right — 45 days
written consent before disclosure
school official exception — vendors under direct control
record of disclosures

commonEvidencePatterns: DPA template establishing Academorix as a school official under direct control
Disclosure log per athlete → parent inspection view
Written parental consent flow for cross-workspace disclosures (rare)

ccpa_cpra.name: California Consumer Privacy Act + California Privacy Rights Act
ccpa_cpra.applicableWhen: Business collecting personal information of California residents (nearly all workspaces have some Californians).

commonArticles.principle: notice at collection
right to delete
right to know categories collected
right to opt-out of sale/sharing
Do Not Sell or Share toggle
sensitive personal information — additional restrictions

pci_dss.name: Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard
pci_dss.strategicPosition: Stay SAQ-A. Card data lives in Stripe Elements iframes; our infrastructure never sees a PAN, CVV, or full track data.
commonEvidencePatterns: No `card_number` / `cvv` / `pan` / `track_data` in any module's schema. Enforced by data-classes.json — the `regulated_financial` tier is reserved for tokenised references only.
Stripe as the SAQ-A boundary. Every subscription module reference is a Stripe customer / subscription id.
Annual SAQ-A + quarterly ASV scan of the marketing surface (out of scope for the app itself).

hipaa_adjacent.name: Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act — adjacent controls
hipaa_adjacent.applicableWhen: Currently NOT applicable — Academorix is not a HIPAA covered entity + no BAA is signed with any workspace. `regulated_health` data-class tier reserved for future v2 when a physical-therapy or sports-medicine vertical lands.
hipaa_adjacent.strategicPosition: Deliberately avoid covered-entity status in v1. Model safeguarding health fields as `regulated_health` data class → encrypted at rest, restricted access, mandatory audit trail, NEVER exported to marketing analytics.

wcag_2_2_aa.name: Web Content Accessibility Guidelines
wcag_2_2_aa.jurisdiction: Public-sector procurement (US Section 508 + EU EN 301 549 + Ontario AODA + Australia DDA)
wcag_2_2_aa.applicableWhen: Every SDUI screen + Refine UI kit component. Public-sector procurement gates on VPAT conformance report.

commonSuccessCriteria.principle: contrast (minimum) — 4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text
link purpose (in context) — link text should indicate destination
focus visible — every focusable element has a visible focus indicator
language of page — <html lang> present + correct
error prevention (legal, financial, data) — confirmable before submission
name, role, value — every interactive element has an accessible name

soc2_type2.name: System and Organization Controls 2 Type II
soc2_type2.applicableWhen: Any enterprise buyer over ~$50k ACV. Independent auditor report over a 12-month audit window.

trustServicesCriteria.principle: control environment — org structure, roles, responsibilities
logical access — least-privilege, role-based, MFA where warranted
system monitoring — health probes, metrics, incident response
system incident response — documented runbooks + evidence
change management — every change ships behind a rollout flag with an audit trail

iso_27001.name: ISO/IEC 27001
iso_27001.jurisdiction: worldwide (customer-driven, especially EU + APAC + Middle East)
iso_27001.applicableWhen: International enterprise buyers request. Substantially overlaps SOC 2 controls with 93 Annex A controls.

can_spam.name: CAN-SPAM Act
can_spam.regulation: 15 U.S.C. §7701 (Controlling the Assault of Non-Solicited Pornography And Marketing Act)
can_spam.applicableWhen: Commercial email under US law. Transactional (invitations, receipts, security alerts) is exempt from the primary requirements but must still comply with accuracy + return-address rules.

commonArticles.principle: accurate header info — From, To, Reply-To, routing headers
accurate subject line — reflects the message content
functional return address AND physical postal address in the footer
unsubscribe mechanism — 10-day honor, at least 30 days functional

casl.name: Canadian Anti-Spam Legislation
casl.applicableWhen: Any electronic message sent to a Canadian recipient.

commonArticles.principle: consent — express or implied
existing business relationship — implied consent for two years post-transaction

eachModuleDeclares: Per-regime: article-level controls it produces evidence for.
Per-control: the concrete evidence artefact (file path, event, retention rule, notification, ...).
downstreamConvention.ciCheck: Every regime + article-level reference in a downstream compliance.json must resolve to an entry defined here. Undefined regimes fail the build.
